metadata.purpose: Section blueprints: per-section goal statement, super-propositions with evidence pointers, dependency graph. Drafted under the ratified Level 1 (cycle 2, commit b73d806): 6 IMRaD moves per D-09 + Amendment 1. Annotate via /section-review level2.
metadata.drafting_note: Section ids S1-S6 are the NEW section set (the 180e2b5 draft's 8 sections map in via mapping_from_180e2b5 per section; status.json's section list is updated to this set when the Level-3 decomposition begins). Evidence typing for S1 follows the completed M1 typing pass recorded in todo/003-references-to-acquire.md (2026-06-06).

schema_example.title: Derivations
schema_example.goal_statement: One paragraph stating exactly what this section must convince the reader of, what it explicitly defers, and which thesis component it serves.

super_propositions.claim: One sentence, falsifiable as stated.
super_propositions.goal: state_result | define_concept | prove_sketch | evaluate_evidence | scope_limit | position_lineage

metadata.amended: 2026-06-06 (cycle 1, in-chat): S1-SP1 attributed / S1-SP3 sharpened (settledness criterion); S4 repaired against the todo/030 DAG (S7 tail on SP1, new SP5b = L5 absorption, SP7 + I7 structural limits); S6-SP6 moved to a title-page declaration (D-11); U2/U3/U5 approved as drafted. Minutes: updates/update-level2-20260606.py.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.mapping_from_180e2b5: Old S1 (sec-intro), rewritten to the four-paragraph funnel; the old intro's lineage material partly moves to S2-SP8.
sections.goal_statement: Convince an applied researcher with quantitative training and working knowledge of MI that (i) the field's own accounts treat MI's major inferential results as settled while LRT and IC are not - and the right question is what 'settled' should mean; (ii) the right yardstick for 'settled' is the complete-data replication principle, posed here at three levels; (iii) this paper resolves criterion replication everywhere and characterizes the exact boundary of decision-rate replication; and (iv) only then, that the derivation is human-prompted AI work under a verification protocol stated as Methods. Defers: all notation and standard results (S2), all proof content (S4), all apparatus (S5). Serves all four thesis components. Style gates: T-06 (every unresolved-status claim typed documented-open vs asserted-here), the todo/003 typing-pass scoping caution (novelty = posing the PRINCIPLE, not the full-data benchmark practice).

super_propositions.claim: The field's own state-of-the-art accounts treat MI's core inferential machinery - mechanisms, point estimation, Wald-type inference - as settled; this maturity claim is reported as theirs, not endorsed, and the introduction's question is what 'settled' should even mean.

Likelihood-ratio testing and information-criterion selection after MI are documented-open: the prior MI-AIC proposal's authors flag its theory as beyond their scope, the first comprehensive selection study calls the literature sparse and averaged criteria unsupported, and the three state-of-the-art accounts contain no IC-after-MI treatment at all.

The complete-data replication principle supplies the missing criterion of settledness - the classical results pass it at the estimate level, which is why they feel settled, while LRT and IC have never been assessed against any stated criterion - posed at three levels (estimates / criterion-in-expectation / decision rates) in the authors' voice, asserted-here: empirical full-data benchmarking is precedented (Wood 2008, C-C 2010), the named principle with an achievability characterization is not.

The averaged log-likelihood overstates its complete-data target by exactly +(1/2)tr(RIV) + (A)+(C), and correcting it restores criterion replication in expectation for any signal strength, while decision-rate replication is complete at the null and provably information-bounded under signal, identically for testing and selection.

This work sits upstream of the Chan calibration line (calibration makes the reference right, this work makes the statistic centered - complementary, not competing) and the trace penalty itself reproduces Shimodaira's AIC_{x;y}, whose prior uses target prediction rather than replication.

The derivation is human-prompted AI work under a stated verification protocol reported in full as the Methods section - one closing paragraph, the paper's only introduction-level statement of the meta layer.

sections.title: AI–human collaboration
sections.mapping_from_180e2b5: NEW section (todo/034 #5, D-12). Pulls the AI-human collaboration framing forward from the Introduction's meta paragraph (S1-P7) and relocates the roles material from the Methods opener (old S3-P2 / S3-SP1). Methods keeps the operational verification detail.
sections.goal_statement: Foreground the AI-human collaboration as a contribution in its own right, between the Introduction and the Background: state that collaboration of this kind is a novelty in the age of AI and that a stated goal of the paper is a workflow that is both productive AND yields accurate conclusions, give the division of labor and the transparent ORCID-verified provenance, and show the collaboration was productive from the coded session record - then hand off to Methods for the safeguards that secure accuracy. Defers all verification detail to S3. Serves the methodological thesis component. Sits low on the technical gradient. Style: plain expository, trust-ordered, no banned vocabulary (T-03).

super_propositions.claim: AI-human collaboration is a novelty in the age of AI, and a stated goal of the paper is a workflow that is both productive (it reaches results a single author would have reached slowly or not at all) and accurate (the results withstand the scrutiny a skeptical referee applies to human-derived mathematics): the human sets direction, standards, field knowledge and acceptance, the AI produces the derivations, drafts and computations, and provenance is transparent and ORCID-verified.

Roles and decision discipline: a human prompter of record set direction and gates, decisions were recorded with rejected options in a dated log, and the AI performed the derivations - with the division of labor stated, not argued.

sections.title: Background and notation
sections.mapping_from_180e2b5: Old S2 (sec-background), reorganized into the three-tier gradient and made dual-purpose (notation established here, once); absorbs lineage detail displaced from the old intro.
sections.goal_statement: Establish the paper's notation and restate every standard result the derivations consume, in the source's own terms, ordered as a deliberate gradient: Tier 1 readable with a calculus-based statistical-inference course, Tier 2 graduate, Tier 3 specialist - so the M1 reader is graduated upward rather than hitting a cliff. Each restatement names the later proposition that depends on it (D-04). Pre-empts the two standard conflations (EM rate matrix vs RIV; observed vs expected information under MAR). Defers: anything novel (S4). Serves the primary thesis component.

super_propositions.claim: Tier 1 - the data decompose as Y = {Y_obs, Y_mis} with missingness indicator R, giving two likelihoods and two MLE targets, theta_com (complete-data) and theta_obs (observed-data), between which this paper's bias lives.
Tier 1 - under MAR with distinct parameters the missingness mechanism is ignorable and R drops out of likelihood inference (Rubin 1976, in his own terms).
Tier 1 - the missing-information principle through Hessians, I_com = I_obs + I_mis|obs, defines the RIV = I_obs^{-1} I_mis|obs that every later result is expressed in.
Tier 2 - EM's Q-function and the MCEM bridge identify MI's averaged log-likelihood as a Monte Carlo Q, the object whose bias the main theorem characterizes.
Tier 2 - Rubin's rules are exact posterior-moment identities given one named approximation, and congeniality is the compatibility condition under which the paper's results hold.
Tier 3 - AIC is a bias-corrected plug-in estimator of expected KL divergence and Wilks gives the LRT's reference; both are stated in the form the missing-data generalizations must reduce to.

Tier 3 - observed and expected information differ under MAR (Kenward-Molenberghs convention adopted, T-02), and the EM rate matrix I_com^{-1} I_mis is NOT the RIV I_obs^{-1} I_mis - the two standard conflations this section exists to prevent.

Tier 3 - the MI test-combining line (Meng-Rubin D-statistics through the Chan calibration) and the prior MI-AIC proposal (Consentino-Claeskens 2010, penalty 2p_S, theory flagged open by its authors) are restated as the launch points for Applications I and II.

sections.title: Methods: the derivation and verification workflow
sections.mapping_from_180e2b5: NEW section (D-09). Absorbs and expands the old S8 provenance material's workflow content; the formal declaration itself stays in S6. Protocol records go to appendices (D-08 note).
sections.goal_statement: State the verification protocol as method - factually, in the passive voice (T-05), one paragraph per part, each gate paragraph ending with what that gate cannot catch - so that 'why believe an AI derivation' is answered as procedure before any result is asked to be believed. The collaboration and roles are described in the preceding AI-human collaboration section (S1B); this section covers the five verification parts (sourced citations, the claim pipeline with trust tiers, preregistration, cross-model adversarial review, reproducibility). The fitted-versus-true-model sign episode is the single narrative passage. Defers: protocol records to appendices, proofs to the companion document, results to S4/S5. Serves the methodological thesis component. Sits deliberately low on the technical gradient (not announced in the text).

super_propositions.claim: Sourced derivation: every external claim was pinned to a source passage in a locally archived, session-read copy, with citation discipline enforced mechanically by a pre-write hook; blind spot - the hook checks existence, not understanding.

Claim pipeline: results were derived, then verified symbolically in two independent computer-algebra systems, then confirmed by Monte Carlo against frozen criteria, with trust tiers (firm / measured / structural / conjecture) assigned by gates cleared; blind spot - dual-CAS catches algebra, not a wrong conditioning shared by both runs.

Preregistration before code: predictions and pass criteria were committed before simulation code, amendments are dated, and failed predictions are reported in the main text; blind spot - preregistration disciplines reporting, it does not validate the predictions' derivations.

Cross-model adversarial verification: load-bearing claims were blindly re-derived by an independent model lineage and subjected to an instructed-to-refute pass with grading records committed; the fitted-vs-oracle episode (8 of 9 blind re-derivations on the opposite sign until the conditioning was pinned) is the worked example of what this gate catches that single-lineage derivation ships.

Reproducibility: seeds, caches, single-entrypoint scripts and environment records allow every number in the paper to be regenerated; blind spot - reproducibility guarantees the numbers, not their interpretation.

sections.title: Derivations
sections.mapping_from_180e2b5: Old S3 (sec-setup) + S4 (sec-lrt) + S5 (sec-ic) become subsections (a)/(b)/(c), prose largely reusable; each subsection gains an opening sentence naming the replication-principle level it addresses; headline numbers stay inline (D-03).
sections.goal_statement: Deliver the paper's analytic content, graded by the trust tiers defined in S3, as the level-by-level answer to the principle posed in S1: (a) the main theorem (criterion replication in expectation), (b) likelihood-ratio comparison (decision-rate replication for testing), (c) information-criterion selection (decision-rate replication for selection). Headline numbers quoted inline at the point of claim; full preregistered account deferred to S5; proofs deferred to the companion document (D-08). Serves primary, secondary and tertiary thesis components. The technical peak of the paper.

super_propositions.claim: Under congenial proper MI with an estimated scale, E[T] = +(1/2)tr(RIV) + (A)+(C): imputation bias +tr(RIV) minus estimation mismatch (1/2)tr(RIV), for the FITTED-imputation Q-bar-infinity (the oracle conditioning flips the sign to -(1/2), stated up front per D-02). The result is confirmed by the convention-free entropy-plug-in reading (E[T] equals the conditional-entropy plug-in bias Delta_n via the entropy-Hessian split - the same bias regrouped).

The result requires an estimated scale (known-scale location-only fits collapse to 0 or -(1/2)tr(RIV)), and the MAR design-imbalance term (A)+(C) carries three-tier standing: sign and order firm, value heavy-tailed O(1), vanishing exactly under MCAR.
Pooled Monte Carlo confirms the theorem: 2.43 +/- 0.26 against the augmented analytic range 2.42-2.55 (quoted inline; full grading in S5).

At the null the LRT numerator's differential bias is tr(RIV_perp) - the tested directions' missing information projected in the I_com metric - and the naive difference of per-model trace corrections ALWAYS overstates it (Gram/Schur; equality only under decoupling; MC-discriminated at 52 se).

Sharing imputations collapses paired noise from O(sqrt n) to O(1) and cancels the (A)+(C) differential exactly at the null; the differential returns at O(1) only for pseudo-true-separated pairs - separation, not nesting, is the criterion.

At the null, tr(RIV_perp) is exactly the mean a correctly calibrated reference distribution absorbs, so adding a numerator correction to a calibrated test double-counts (W2: size 0.042 uncorrected vs 0.034 over-corrected) - the bias therefore matters precisely where no reference stands between the statistic and its use: information-criterion ranking, explicit numerator corrections, and non-nested comparison.

Adding tr(RIV_k) to MI-AIC reproduces Shimodaira's AIC_{x;y} penalty and removes the systematic tilt toward high-missing-information candidates (100% of misclassifications land on the largest-RIV candidate in the preregistered congenial cells).

The calibration ladder splits the achievable target along the null/noncentral axis: rungs 2-3 select indistinguishably from complete-data AIC on the null side (0.904/0.903 vs 0.899), while noncentral statistics shrink by exactly predicted factors and the weak-signal floor (0.17) is information loss, not calibration error. Two structural limits qualify generalization: the W3/ladder candidate family is exactly the naive-trace equality case (the overstatement is invisible there, naive vs exact agreement 3.6e-15), and per-model marginal transforms cannot calibrate a difference distribution (score-copula invariance; equated Delta mean 6.10 vs complete-data 10.00).
Each subsection opens by naming the replication-principle level it addresses, so the S1 question is answered piece by piece in the reader's sight.

sections.title: Simulation studies
sections.mapping_from_180e2b5: Old S6 (sec-numerics), restructured into the two subheads 'Simulation studies: setup' and 'Simulation studies: results' (D-09 Amendment 1); content largely reusable.
sections.goal_statement: Present the consolidated pre-registered evidence as a single auditable unit: setup states the apparatus once (designs, engines, repetition counts, seeds, preregistration protocol and amendment history - 'repetitions', never 'replications', per T-07); results grade every frozen prediction including the failures, prominently (D-05). Steps back down the technical gradient from S4. Serves all four thesis components (the methodological one through the preregistration narrative).

super_propositions.claim: Setup - the full apparatus is stated once: MVN designs, missingness mechanisms, MI engines, repetition counts and seeds, with every study preregistered before code and amendments dated.

Results - the frozen predictions grade out: W1 bias decomposition, the W2 differential, the W3/ladder selection results (C1-C6 pass), and the non-nested W4 measurements, each tied to its committed artifact.
Failures are reported as a unit in the main text: the similar-pair differential below resolution, the N=1000 cell at 2.6 se, and the component-level 9-20% misses (D-05).

Engine sensitivity is scoped: EMB/Amelia agrees with the engine-free observed-data RIV within 2-3% and the selection results within 0.011, so the conclusions are not artifacts of one imputation engine - an empirical, not analytic, robustness statement.

sections.title: Discussion
sections.mapping_from_180e2b5: Old S7 (sec-discussion), reordered to the inverse gradient with the plain-language verdict as item (4) and the workflow reflection as the close. Old S8 (sec-provenance) is split: substance -> S3 Methods; formal declaration -> title-page declaration block (D-11); nothing of it remains as section content.
sections.goal_statement: Close the paper on the inverse gradient: open at the technical level the derivations earned (G1-G7 next to the claims they qualify, the power-dominance conjecture labeled), then broaden until the closing passages - the plain-language replication-principle verdict and the workflow reflection - are fully readable by the S1 reader, ending on the workflow reflection. NO separate Conclusion section; the verdict paragraph is the takeaway block. The JAIGP provenance declaration is NOT section content - it lives in a title-page declaration block (D-11). Serves all four thesis components.

super_propositions.claim: G1-G7 scope limits are stated next to the claims they qualify, each with its evidential standing (general statement / MVN verification per D-07 among them).
Power dominance of the corrected statistic is stated as a conjecture with partial empirical support (W2), not as a result.
Three future directions: covariate shift (Shimodaira's named future work), exact-engine replication of the bias constants, and selection-aware calibration (the sequel, developed separately per D-06).

The plain-language verdict: after correction, the criterion matches complete data on average; at the null, selection and testing behave as if the data were complete; under signal, missing information costs power that no post-processing buys back - and the open piece is exactly the sequel's subject.
The workflow reflection is itself trust-ordered: what the gates demonstrably caught (the sign episode), what they cost, and what they cannot catch - demonstrated claims separated from asserted ones.

metrics_gate.notes: AI-human collaboration section S1B inserted 2026-06-27 (todo/034 #5, D-12): roles super-proposition relocated from S3 (now S1B-SP2) plus a new framing SP (S1B-SP1); S3 drops to five verification parts and now depends_on S1B; DAG re-checked acyclic. Prior note: Re-evaluated after cycle 1 (2026-06-06): 38 super-propositions (S4 gained SP5b, S6 lost SP6 to the title page); all evidence-pointed; DAG unchanged and acyclic; every section serves a ratified arc move. S4 checked against the todo/030 argument map: C1-C5, S1-S7, L2-L5, I4-I7 all carried (S7 in SP1's tail, L5 as SP5b, I7 in SP7); map itself lags D-09/D-10 - sync queued as follow-up.

front_matter.title_page_declaration: Authorship and provenance declaration (D-11), title-page block per scholarly declaration conventions: AI lineage and role in the derivation, ORCID-verified human prompter of record (marcus.waldman), pointer to the public repository and the companion derivation document. The reader knows the paper's nature before page one; M1's closing meta paragraph elaborates what the title page declares.
